title: Mobility limitation documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports documenting mobility, gait, balance, muscle strength, function, footwear, neurological, vision, hearing, cognition, medicines and support context without assigning cause or recommending equipment.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
source.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

assessment.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
assessment.rationale: The exact section supports independence, social participation, carer involvement and clinician-entered planning.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
source.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
source.setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
